1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul, an apostle. I was not sent by people or chosen by any person. I was sent by Jesus Christ and by God the Father, who raised Jesus from death.
All the brothers and sisters here with me join me in writing. To the churches in Galatia:
May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
Jesus gave himself for our sins to rescue us from this present evil world. He did this because God our Father wanted it.
May God have glory forever and ever. Amen.
I am shocked that you are turning away so quickly from the One who called you by the grace of Christ. You are turning to a different “good news” —
which is not good news at all. Some people are confusing you and trying to twist the good news about Christ.
But even if we, or an angel from heaven, were to preach a different “good news” from the one we preached to you, let that person be cursed.
I have said it before, and I say it again: if anyone preaches to you a “good news” different from the one you received, let that person be cursed.
Am I now trying to win people's approval, or God's? Am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I would not be a servant of Christ.
Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the good news I preached does not come from any human being.
I did not receive it from a person, and no one taught it to me. Jesus Christ himself showed it to me.
You have heard how I lived before, in the Jewish religion. I attacked God's church without mercy and tried to destroy it.
In the Jewish religion I was ahead of many people my own age, because I worked so hard to follow the traditions of my ancestors.
But God had set me apart even before I was born, and he called me by his grace. When God was pleased to
show his Son to me, so that I could preach about him among people who are not Jews, I did not ask any human being for advice.
I did not even go up to Jerusalem to see those who were apostles before me. Instead, I went away to Arabia, and later I returned to Damascus.
Three years later I went up to Jerusalem to meet Peter, and I stayed with him fifteen days.
I did not see any of the other apostles, except James, the Lord's brother.
I promise you, before God, that what I am writing to you is true. I am not lying.
Later I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
The churches of Christ in Judea did not know me by sight.
They only kept hearing people say: The man who used to attack us is now preaching the faith he once tried to destroy.
And they praised God because of me.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Fourteen years later, I went up to Jerusalem again, this time with Barnabas. I also took Titus with me.
I went because God showed me I should go. I explained to them the good news that I preach among the people who are not Jews. I spoke privately with the leaders, so that my work would not be wasted.
Yet they did not even force Titus, who was with me, to be circumcised, even though he was a Greek.
This question came up because some false brothers had slipped in among us. They came secretly to spy on the freedom we have in Christ Jesus, so that they could make us slaves again.
But we did not give in to them, not even for a moment, so that the truth of the good news would stay with you.
As for the leaders who seemed important — whatever they were makes no difference to me, because God does not judge by how important a person seems — those leaders added nothing to my message.
Instead, they saw that God had trusted me to take the good news to people who are not Jews, just as he had trusted Peter to take it to the Jews.
For God, who worked through Peter as an apostle to the Jews, also worked through me as an apostle to the other nations.

James, Peter, and John, who were seen as pillars of the church, recognized the grace God had given me. So they shook hands with me and Barnabas as partners. We would go to the other nations, and they would go to the Jews.
They asked only one thing: that we keep remembering the poor — the very thing I was eager to do.
But when Peter came to Antioch, I told him to his face that he was wrong.

Here is what happened: before certain men came from James, Peter used to eat with the people who were not Jews. But when those men arrived, Peter pulled back and stopped eating with them, because he was afraid of the Jews.
The other Jewish believers joined Peter in this pretending, so that even Barnabas was led astray.

When I saw that they were not being honest about the truth of the good news, I said to Peter in front of everyone: You are a Jew, yet you live like the other nations and not like a Jew. So why are you forcing the other nations to live like Jews?
We are Jews by birth, not “sinners from the other nations.”

Yet we know that no one is made right with God by obeying the law. A person is made right only by faith in Jesus Christ. So we too have put our faith in Christ Jesus, to be made right with God by faith in Christ and not by obeying the law. For no one will be made right with God by obeying the law.
But suppose we are trying to be made right with God through Christ, and it becomes clear that we ourselves are sinners. Does that mean Christ leads people into sin? No, never!
If I build again what I tore down, I show that I am a lawbreaker.
For through the law I died to the law, so that I could live for God.
I have been put to death on the cross with Christ. I no longer live, but Christ lives in me. The life I now live in my body, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself for me.
I do not throw away the grace of God. For if a person could be made right with God by the law, then Christ died for nothing.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: You foolish people of Galatia! Who has put a spell on you? Jesus Christ was clearly shown to you as the one who died on the cross.
I want to learn just one thing from you: Did you receive the Spirit by obeying the law, or by believing what you heard?
Are you so foolish? You began with the Spirit. Are you now trying to finish by your own human effort?
Have you suffered so much for nothing — if it really was for nothing?
So when God gives you his Spirit and does miracles among you, does he do it because you obey the law, or because you believe what you heard?
It is the same as with Abraham: He believed God, and because of that he was accepted as right with God.
So you can see that the true children of Abraham are the people who have faith.

The Scripture knew ahead of time that God would make the other nations right with him through faith. So the Scripture told the good news to Abraham in advance: All nations will be blessed through you.
So those who have faith are blessed together with Abraham, the man of faith.
But all who depend on obeying the law are under a curse. For the Scripture says: Cursed is everyone who does not keep doing everything written in the book of the law.
Clearly, no one is made right with God by the law, because the Scripture says: The person who is right with God will live by faith.
The law is not based on faith. Instead it says: The person who does these things will live by them.
Christ set us free from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us. For the Scripture says: Cursed is everyone who is hung on a tree.
Christ did this so that the blessing given to Abraham could come to the other nations through Jesus Christ, and so that by faith we could receive the Spirit that God promised.
Brothers and sisters, let me use an everyday example. Once an agreement between people is signed, no one can cancel it or add to it.
Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his seed. The Scripture does not say “and to seeds,” meaning many people. It says “and to your seed,” meaning one person, who is Christ.
Here is what I mean: the law came four hundred and thirty years later. It cannot cancel the agreement God had already made, and so it cannot do away with the promise.
For if what God gives depends on the law, then it no longer depends on the promise. But God gave it to Abraham as a promise, by his grace.
Then what was the law for? It was added because of sin, until the Seed should come — the one to whom the promise was made. The law was given through angels, by a go-between.
Now a go-between is for more than one side, but God is one.
So is the law against the promises of God? No, never! If a law had been given that could give life, then being right with God really would come through the law.
But the Scripture says that the whole world is a prisoner of sin, so that the promise might be given — through faith in Jesus Christ — to those who believe.
Before this faith came, we were held prisoner by the law, locked up until the coming faith would be shown to us.
So the law was like a guardian to lead us to Christ, so that we could be made right with God by faith.
Now that this faith has come, we are no longer under a guardian.
So in Christ Jesus you are all children of God through faith.
For all of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
There is no longer Jew or Greek, slave or free, male or female. You are all one in Christ Jesus.
And if you belong to Christ, then you are Abraham's family, and you will receive what God promised.

4.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Here is what I mean. As long as an heir is a child, he is no different from a servant, even though he owns everything.
He is under guardians and managers until the time his father has set.
It is the same with us. When we were children, we were slaves to the basic rules of the world.
But when the right time came, God sent his Son, born of a woman, born under the law,
to set free those who were under the law, so that we could be adopted as God's children.
And because you are his children, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, the Spirit who cries out: Abba, Father.
So you are no longer a slave, but a child. And since you are his child, God has made you an heir.
In the past, when you did not know God, you were slaves to things that are not really gods at all.
But now you know God — or rather, God knows you. So how can you turn back again to those weak and useless rules? Do you want to be their slaves all over again?
You are keeping special days and months and seasons and years!
I am afraid for you. I am afraid that my hard work for you may have been wasted.
Brothers and sisters, I beg you, become like me, for I became like you. You did me no wrong.
You remember that it was because I was sick that I first preached the good news to you.
My illness was hard for you, but you did not look down on me or turn away from me. Instead, you welcomed me as if I were an angel of God, as if I were Christ Jesus himself.
You were so happy then. What happened to that joy? I can say this about you: if it had been possible, you would have torn out your own eyes and given them to me.
So have I now become your enemy just because I tell you the truth?
Those other people are eager to win you over, but not for good reasons. They want to shut you out, so that you will be eager for them.
It is good to be eager, as long as the reason is good — and not only when I am there with you.
My dear children, I am again in pain for you, like a mother giving birth, until Christ is formed in you.
How I wish I could be with you now and speak to you in a gentler way, because I do not know what to do about you.
Tell me, you who want to be under the law: do you not hear what the law says?
The Scripture says that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and one by the free woman.
The son of the slave woman was born in the ordinary way, but the son of the free woman was born because of God's promise.
These things are a picture of something more. The two women stand for two agreements. One agreement comes from Mount Sinai, and it gives birth to children who are slaves. This is Hagar.
Hagar stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia, and she is like the Jerusalem of today, which is a slave together with her children.
But the Jerusalem that is above is free, and she is our mother.
For the Scripture says: Be glad, you woman who could not have children. Shout for joy, you who never gave birth. For the lonely woman has more children than the woman who has a husband.
Now you, brothers and sisters, are children of God's promise, just like Isaac.
Long ago, the son born in the ordinary way treated the son born by the Spirit badly. It is the same now.
But what does the Scripture say? Send the slave woman and her son away! For the slave woman's son will never share what belongs to the free woman's son.
So then, brothers and sisters, we are not children of the slave woman, but of the free woman.

5.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Christ set us free so that we could be free. So stand firm, and do not let yourselves be tied up again as slaves.
Listen! I, Paul, tell you that if you let yourselves be circumcised to be saved, then Christ is no help to you at all.
Again I warn every man who gets circumcised for that reason: he must then obey the whole law.
If you try to be made right with God by the law, you have cut yourselves off from Christ; you have fallen away from grace.
But by the Spirit we wait, with hope, to be made right with God through faith.
For in Christ Jesus it does not matter whether a person is circumcised or not. What matters is faith that works through love.
You were running so well. Who got in your way and stopped you from obeying the truth?
That kind of teaching does not come from the One who called you.
Just a little yeast spreads through the whole batch of dough.
But because of the Lord, I am sure that you will not think any other way. And the one who is confusing you will be judged, whoever he is.
Brothers and sisters, if I were still preaching that people must be circumcised, why am I still being attacked? If that were true, the cross would no longer offend anyone.
As for those who are upsetting you — I wish they would go all the way and cut themselves off!
You were called to be free, brothers and sisters. But do not use your freedom as an excuse to follow your sinful self. Instead, serve one another in love.
For the whole law is summed up in one command: Love your neighbor as yourself.
But if you keep biting and tearing at each other, watch out, or you will destroy one another.
So I say, let the Spirit lead your life, and you will not do what your sinful self wants.
For the sinful self wants what is against the Spirit, and the Spirit wants what is against the sinful self. They fight each other, so that you do not do what you want to do.
But if the Spirit leads you, you are not under the law.
The things the sinful self does are easy to see: sexual sin, anything unclean, wild living,
worshiping idols, witchcraft, hate, fighting, jealousy, bursts of anger, selfish ambition, arguing, splitting into groups,
envy, drunkenness, wild parties, and things like these. I warn you, as I warned you before: people who live like this will not be part of God's kingdom.
But the fruit that the Spirit grows is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against things like these.
Those who belong to Christ Jesus have nailed their sinful self to the cross, with all its desires.
Since we live by the Spirit, let us also follow the Spirit.
Let us not be proud, making each other angry and jealous.

6.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Brothers and sisters, if someone is caught in a sin, then you who live by the Spirit should gently help that person back. And be careful, or you also may be tempted.
Carry one another's heavy loads, and in this way you will obey the law of Christ.
If anyone thinks he is something when he is nothing, he is only fooling himself.
Each person should look closely at his own work. Then he can be proud of what he himself has done, without comparing himself to anyone else.
For each person must carry his own load.
Anyone who is being taught the word should share all good things with the teacher.
Do not be fooled: no one can make fun of God. People harvest exactly what they plant.
Whoever plants to please his sinful self will harvest ruin from that self. But whoever plants to please the Spirit will harvest eternal life from the Spirit.
So let us not get tired of doing good. At the right time we will have a harvest, if we do not give up.
So whenever we have the chance, let us do good to everyone, and especially to those who belong to the family of believers.
See what large letters I am using as I write to you with my own hand!
Those who are forcing you to be circumcised are only trying to look good on the outside. They do it so that they will not be attacked because of the cross of Christ.
Even those who are circumcised do not obey the law themselves. They want you to be circumcised so that they can brag about your bodies.
But I will never brag about anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. Through the cross, the world has been put to death to me, and I to the world.
For it does not matter whether a person is circumcised or not. What matters is being a new creation.
May peace and mercy be on all who live by this rule, and on the Israel of God.
From now on, let no one give me trouble, for I carry on my body the scars that show I belong to Jesus.
Brothers and sisters, may the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
